title: Mangrove Forests
description: For questions 9 – 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Mangrove forests are unique coastal ecosystems (0) that thrive at the boundary between land and sea. These remarkable trees have adapted to survive in harsh conditions, including salt water, unstable soil, and tidal flooding that would kill most other plants.||Mangroves are found (9) in tropical and subtropical regions around the world, with the largest forests located in Southeast Asia. The trees have developed special root systems (10) that can filter salt from seawater and anchor them firmly in muddy soil. Some species also have aerial roots that stick (11) out of the mud, allowing them to breathe even when submerged during high tide.||These forests provide crucial environmental services. They protect coastlines (12) from erosion and storm damage by absorbing wave energy. During tsunamis and hurricanes, mangrove forests have (13) been shown to significantly reduce damage to coastal communities. The tangled root systems create nursery habitats for fish, shrimp, and other marine species, supporting both biodiversity and local fishing industries.||Mangroves are also powerful carbon sinks, storing (14) more carbon per unit area than most tropical forests. Unfortunately, over half of the world's mangrove forests (15) have been destroyed in recent decades, cleared for coastal development, aquaculture, and agriculture. Conservation organisations are working (16) to restore mangrove forests, recognising that protecting these ecosystems benefits both people and nature.

example.text: Mangrove forests are unique coastal ecosystems (0) thrive at the boundary between land and sea.

questions.explanation: Preposition 'in' indicates location in geographical regions.
Relative pronoun referring to root systems.
Part of the phrasal verb 'stick out of', meaning to protrude or extend beyond a surface.
Verb 'protect from' indicates protection against erosion and damage.
Present perfect passive: 'have been shown' indicates demonstration.
Comparativo: 'more carbon...than' compara almacenamiento de carbono.
Present perfect passive: 'have been destroyed' with a plural subject.
Expression 'working to restore' indicates the goal of the work.
